title: Papercut Dispatcher
lede: Convert user-reported UI issues directly into draft pull requests. Assign AI agents to handle the visual polish so engineering can focus on the core architecture.

sections.title: Triage Queue
sections.description: Recent support tickets flagged as UX paper cuts. Review the reported behaviour and dispatch an AI agent to generate a fix.
rows: Hover state colour missing on primary nav
Tooltip overlaps with submit button on mobile
Inconsistent date format in billing table
sections.emptyMessage: No pending paper cuts in the queue.

sections.title: Active Agent Activity
sections.description: Live status of AI agents currently drafting pull requests based on support feedback.
items: Drafting fix for #8488: Aligning table columns in dashboard view
Running local UI tests for #8472: Correcting mobile padding on login screen
PR #102 opened for #8450: Updating legacy icon assets

sections.title: Agent Configuration & Safeguards
sections.body: AI agents are currently constrained to frontend display logic, CSS variables, and copy changes. Modifications to core database schemas or authentication logic will automatically fail the dispatch process and require standard engineering escalation.

demoSteps: Review the Triage Queue for recent customer complaints regarding UI behaviour.
Click 'Dispatch AI' on a pending ticket to assign a junior engineering agent to the task.
Monitor the Agent Activity list to see the AI draft the pull request and run initial tests.
notes: Ensure UK spelling across all UI copy (e.g., colour, behaviour).
The demo relies on static dummy data representing support tickets; no live Zendesk or GitHub integration is present.
Assume the user has the authority to dispatch AI agents but not merge code directly to production.
